title: Understand: The Original Constitution
description: Check your understanding of the articles and sections in the Constitution in this set of free practice questions.
topic_path: Civil Dialogue, The Constitution, and Constitutional Interpretation

choices.content: The supreme law of the land in America
A source of much debate and discussion among Americans
A document declaring independence from Great Britain
A suggestion of rules for the government to follow

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Constitution is not a particularly long document. Which of the following statements is true about why the founders wrote it that way?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wealthy elites should own a copy in their household.
Everyone should save money on the ink needed to print it.
Everyone should be able to read it and understand it.
New laws should be confusing and difficult to understand.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which phrase in the Preamble to the Constitution is a powerful expression of one of its core principles, popular sovereignty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Four score and seven years ago
We hold these truths to be self-evident
In Order to form a more perfect Union

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Supreme Court Justice Oliver Wendell Holmes, who was the Constitution made for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People who can always agree on everything
Only those who had served in the military

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete this 1787 quotation from George Washington.**

“I do not conceive that we are more inspired—have more wisdom—or possess more virtue than those who will come after us. The power under the Constitution will always be with  \_\_\_\_:"

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which THREE parts of the U.S. Constitution establish the three branches of government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Where is the Supremacy Clause in the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Article IV includes the Privileges and Immunities Clause. What does this clause guarantee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: That the Constitution is the supreme law of the land, meaning that states must follow the Constitution
That the civil rights of citizens follow them when they travel to other states
That government officials are protected from prosecution when they violate the law
That the people have a way to change the Constitution through the amendment process

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which part of the Constitution establishes that the U.S. Constitution is the supreme law of the land?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Why is the Supremacy Clause important?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It establishes that federal law comes before state law when there are conflicts.
It establishes the office of the president and the executive branch.
It states that all laws must come from Congress.
It states how the people can change the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which part of the U.S. Constitution describes how new states are added?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Where in the U.S. Constitution did the framers promote the principle of judicial independence?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
